sections.paragraph:
ἄτεκνος, ον (s. τέκνον; Hes.+; Polyb. 20, 6, 5; Plut.; Lucian; ins [SIG2 838, 6: II b.c.; 858, 13; epitaphs 11 and 22, 7: ZNW 22, 1923, 281; 283]; pap [PLond I, 23, 13 p. 38; s. also Preis.]; LXX; En 98:5; TestJud 8:3; 19:2; Philo in Eus., PE 8, 11, 13; Jos., Bell. 1, 563, Ant. 4, 254) childless Lk 20:28f. Subst. ἡ ἄτεκνος GJs 4:4 (cp. Is 49:21).—DELG s.v. τίκτω. M-M.

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Preis. Preis. = FPreisigke, Wörterbuch der griech. Papyrusurkunden—Lists 4, 6

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 148
